coverage_note: SIK3 population interpretation currently depends more on cohort-specific association studies and rare-variant reports than on a mature embedded allele-frequency panel.
gene_population_patterns_intro: Broader population patterns curated from SIK3 sleep, hearing, and metabolic-signaling literature.

sources.label: PubMed 32126566: SIK3 haplotypes and hearing-loss risk
DOI 10.1073/pnas.2500356122: SIK3-N783Y and natural short sleep

gene_population_patterns.summary: Published common-variant SIK3 literature is still cohort specific, including hearing-loss and adiposity studies, so the locus is best interpreted as emerging rather than fully settled population genetics.

The most striking human SIK3 result to date is a rare functional sleep-trait variant, underscoring that this gene is currently driven by mechanistic and rare-variant evidence more than by broad common-SNP panels.
